Free & Non-Profit Resources — No cost to families
National Free Resources
Wrightslaw
Free guides on special education law, IEPs, and parent rights under IDEA.
Parent Training & Information Centers (PTI)
Federally funded free training and information for families of children with disabilities.
PACER Center
Free resources, workshops, and publications on special education and disability rights.
National Center for Learning Disabilities
Research, advocacy, and free toolkits for families of students with learning disabilities.
IDA (Dyslexia Handbook)
Free dyslexia fact sheets, screenings, and support for parents and educators.
Texas State Resources
SPEDTex
Texas Education Agency's free parent support line and resource library for special education.
Disability Rights Texas
Free legal advocacy and self-advocacy resources for Texans with disabilities.
Texas Parent to Parent
Connects Texas families of children with disabilities with trained volunteer parents.
Texas Workforce Commission – VR
Free vocational rehabilitation services for students with disabilities transitioning to work.
Texas School for the Blind & Visually Impaired
Free outreach and resources for students with visual impairments across Texas.
Local Resources — Abilene Area
Region 14 Education Service Center
Provides support and resources for special education services in the region, including Abilene.
Abilene ISD Special Education Department
Information and resources regarding special education services offered within Abilene ISD.
Disability Rights Texas
Offers legal advocacy and support for Texans with disabilities, including special education issues.
